title: Ransomware Group Claims 2.5 Million Records Stolen from Healthcare Provider
summary: The ALPHV/BlackCat ransomware group has listed a major US healthcare network on its leak site, claiming to have exfiltrated 2.5 million patient records including PII and insurance data. The organisation has yet to confirm or deny the breach publicly.

title: Critical Auth Bypass in Fortinet FortiOS Exploited in the Wild — Patch Now
summary: CISA has added CVE-2026-11432 (CVSS 9.8) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalogue after confirmed in-the-wild exploitation against government and critical infrastructure targets. Fortinet has issued an emergency patch for FortiOS 7.4.x and earlier.

title: PowerShell Stealer Targeting VS Code Extensions Spreads via npm Packages
summary: A newly observed malware campaign is distributing a PowerShell-based credential stealer through trojanised npm packages that mimic popular VS Code extension helpers. The packages have been downloaded over 40,000 times before detection and removal.

title: CISA Releases Advisory on Volt Typhoon Pre-Positioning in US Critical Infrastructure
summary: CISA, NSA, and FBI have released a joint advisory detailing tactics, techniques, and procedures used by Volt Typhoon (PRC state-sponsored) to establish persistent access inside US energy, water, and transportation networks. Includes 17 specific detection rules.

title: North Korea's Lazarus Group Targets Crypto Developers with Fake Job Offers
summary: Researchers at SentinelOne have documented a new Lazarus Group campaign sending fake recruitment PDFs that execute a multi-stage loader when opened. Targets are primarily senior developers at DeFi protocols and centralised exchanges.

title: The Invisible Attack Surface: When AI Agents Browse the Web
summary: A detailed analysis of prompt injection risks in agentic AI systems that interact with external web content. As LLM-powered agents are given more autonomy over browsers and APIs, adversarially crafted web pages can hijack their behaviour without user awareness.

title: In-Depth: Exploiting a Heap Use-After-Free in the Linux io_uring Subsystem
summary: Project Zero publishes a full writeup of a heap UAF vulnerability in the Linux kernel's io_uring subsystem (CVE-2025-49821, CVSS 7.8) including a working privilege escalation PoC on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS. Patch landed in kernel 6.14.2.

title: Mustang Panda's New Loader Evades EDR via COM Hijacking and BYOVD
summary: Cisco Talos has published research on a new variant of Mustang Panda's PlugX loader that uses COM object hijacking combined with a bring-your-own-vulnerable-driver technique to disable Windows Defender before dropping its final payload.

title: Sandworm Deploys Wiper Against Ukrainian Energy Grid — Third Campaign This Winter
summary: Google Threat Intelligence Group has attributed a destructive wiper attack against three Ukrainian energy distribution companies to Sandworm (APT44). The malware, dubbed FROSTBITE, targets industrial control systems and overwrites bootloaders before activating.

title: Scattered Spider's New Playbook: SIM Swapping at Scale via Insider Recruitment
summary: Palo Alto Unit 42 has tracked a shift in Scattered Spider TTPs — the group is now systematically recruiting telecom insiders via encrypted messaging apps to perform SIM swaps on high-value corporate executives, bypassing MFA on M365 and Okta.

title: ENISA Threat Landscape 2026: AI-Assisted Attacks Now in Top 5 for First Time
summary: ENISA's annual threat landscape report ranks AI-assisted cyberattacks among the top five threats for the first time, alongside ransomware, supply chain attacks, social engineering, and DDoS. The report highlights a 340% rise in AI-generated spear-phishing campaigns.

title: PyPI Maintainers Remove 47 Packages Distributing Claude API Key Stealer
summary: PyPI's security team has removed 47 packages that contained obfuscated code designed to exfiltrate Anthropic API keys and local .env files to an attacker-controlled server. The packages used typosquatting on popular AI client libraries.
